other_observers: Bill & JoAnn Hackos, David Leatherman, Todd Deininger, and several others on the CFO field trip.; I found the bird.
specific_location: On the west side of South River Road about 2-3 miles north of the Conejos County line.
description: The bird was clearly an ibis, a dark-plumaged, medium-sized wading bird with a long, thick, downcurved bill. Its bill was a basic horn gray color, and the facial skin adjacent to the bill was dark, possibly with a bluish cast, but without any red tones. Its eye was similarly dark. A bright electric blue line ran along the upper edge of the facial skin from the bill to the top of the eye, and another similar blue line ran from the base of the bill along the bottom of the facial skin to the bottom of the eye. The bird's legs were grayish with an extensive red wash around the "knees" and adjacent parts of the legs.Behaviors: The bird was foraging with 6-8 White-faced Ibis in a wet meadow dominated by thick lush grass that came up almost to the bird's belly. Luckily, this individual had a very helpful habit of lifting its legs high over the tops of the grass when it wanted to move around, which greatly helped our views of leg color.Call: Bird was silent.Plumage: Adult alternate
